pbq.title: Windows application support tickets
pbq.scenario: A help desk technician is triaging three Windows tickets that started after routine updates.

items.label: Ticket A
items.value: Line-of-business app crashes only for one user profile

items.label: Ticket B
items.value: Laptop boots to recovery after repeated failed startup attempts

items.label: Ticket C
items.value: Browser redirects search results and shows unexpected extensions

items.label: Ticket A
items.value: Same app works under a newly created local test account

items.label: Ticket B
items.value: Startup Repair is available from Windows Recovery Environment

items.label: Ticket C
items.value: Endpoint protection shows a potentially unwanted program alert

question: Match each support ticket to the best next action.
itemsLeft: Application crashes only inside one damaged user profile
System repeatedly fails startup and enters recovery
Browser redirects and unwanted extensions appear after a PUP alert
itemsRight: Run antimalware cleanup, remove suspicious extensions, and reset browser settings
Use WinRE Startup Repair or restore options before reinstalling the OS
Repair the user profile or migrate data to a new profile after confirming app health
explanation: A profile-only crash should be handled as profile corruption after confirming the app works elsewhere. Repeated boot failure belongs in WinRE repair workflow. Redirects with unwanted extensions and a PUP alert call for malware/PUP cleanup and browser reset.

pbq.title: Endpoint security response ticket
pbq.scenario: A technician is asked to respond to three security events on end-user systems without losing evidence or spreading the issue.

items.label: Laptop A
items.value: User clicked a phishing link and entered credentials

items.label: Laptop B
items.value: Ransom note appears and files are actively being renamed

items.label: Laptop C
items.value: Lost device contains cached corporate email

question: Match each endpoint security event to the best immediate response.
itemsLeft: A user submitted credentials to a confirmed phishing page
A system shows active ransomware-like file changes
A managed laptop with cached email is reported lost
itemsRight: Isolate the host from the network and escalate according to incident response procedures
Trigger remote lock or wipe and document the asset incident
Reset the password, revoke sessions/tokens, and report the account compromise
explanation: Phished credentials require account containment, password/session reset, and reporting. Active ransomware behavior should be isolated immediately. A lost managed device should be locked or wiped according to policy and documented.

pbq.title: Windows account access ticket
pbq.scenario: A help desk technician triages three user access problems. Match each symptom to the best OS-level action.

items.label: User A
items.value: Forgot local password on a domain-joined laptop

items.label: User B
items.value: Needs temporary admin rights for approved installer

items.label: User C
items.value: Profile loads with temporary profile warning

question: Match each access issue to the best next action.
itemsLeft: User needs temporary elevated rights for approved software
Windows loads a temporary profile warning
itemsRight: Reset the domain account password through approved identity tools
Use approved elevation/admin workflow and remove rights after the task
Repair/recreate the profile and migrate user data as appropriate
explanation: Domain password issues are handled through identity tooling. Temporary admin access should follow least privilege and approval. Temporary profiles indicate profile corruption or load failure.

pbq.title: Disk maintenance ticket
pbq.scenario: Three Windows systems report storage-related issues. Match each clue to the best tool or action.

items.label: PC A
items.value: Disk is nearly full with old update/cache files

items.label: PC B
items.value: File system errors after an improper shutdown

items.label: PC C
items.value: Needs partition resized before encryption rollout

question: Match each storage issue to the best action.
itemsLeft: Recover space from old cache/update files
Check and repair file system errors
Resize a partition before enabling encryption
itemsRight: Run Disk Cleanup/Storage Sense with approved cleanup categories
Run chkdsk or equivalent file-system repair workflow
Use Disk Management or approved partitioning tool
explanation: Cleanup tools remove safe temporary/update files. File-system errors call for repair checks. Partition size/layout changes belong in disk management tooling.

pbq.title: Malware response ticket
pbq.scenario: A technician responds to three suspicious endpoint behaviors. Match each to the best containment or remediation step.

items.label: Host A
items.value: Antivirus disabled and unknown service persists

items.label: Host B
items.value: User sees fake antivirus pop-ups

items.label: Host C
items.value: Sensitive browser sessions remain open after compromise

question: Match each malware-response clue to the best step.
itemsLeft: Unknown persistent service with disabled protection
Fake antivirus pop-ups pressure the user to pay
Open sessions remain after suspected account compromise
itemsRight: Isolate, re-enable/update protection, scan offline if needed, and remove persistence
Remove rogue software/browser changes and educate user not to pay/click
Revoke sessions/tokens and reset affected credentials
explanation: Persistent malware should be isolated and scanned with trusted tools. Scareware/rogue AV requires removal and user coaching. Active sessions after compromise must be revoked.

pbq.title: Mobile OS support ticket
pbq.scenario: Three mobile users report application or OS issues. Match each symptom to the best support action.

items.label: Phone A
items.value: App crashes after update; other apps work

items.label: Phone B
items.value: Corporate mail stops after password change

items.label: Phone C
items.value: Device is slow and storage is almost full

question: Match each mobile support issue to the best action.
itemsLeft: One app crashes immediately after it was updated
Corporate mail fails after password change
Device performance is poor with nearly full storage
itemsRight: Clear app cache/reinstall or roll back through approved app management
Update saved credentials or re-enroll mail profile if required
Free storage by removing cached/offline data and unused apps
explanation: A single-app crash points to app cache/reinstall. Password changes often require updated mail credentials or profile refresh. Low storage can degrade mobile performance.

pbq.title: Change-control ticket
pbq.scenario: A junior technician is preparing three maintenance actions. Match each action to the right operational procedure.

items.label: Patch
items.value: Server restart required during business hours

items.label: Repair
items.value: Customer data may be visible on screen

items.label: Install
items.value: New software could affect a shared workstation image

question: Match each work item to the correct procedure.
itemsLeft: Customer data may be visible during repair
Software install may affect a shared image
itemsRight: Get approval, communicate impact, and schedule a maintenance window
Protect privacy by minimizing exposure and following data-handling policy
Test/approve the change before adding it to the standard image
explanation: Service-impacting restarts need approval and communication. Visible customer data requires privacy controls. Shared images need tested and approved changes.

pbq.title: Authentication hardening ticket
pbq.scenario: A small office is improving authentication. Match each requirement to the best control.

items.label: Remote access
items.value: Needs stronger sign-in than password only

items.label: Shared workstation
items.value: Needs unique accountability

items.label: Privileged task
items.value: Needs admin rights only during approved work

question: Match each authentication requirement to the best control.
itemsLeft: Shared workstation actions need user accountability
Admin rights should exist only during approved tasks
itemsRight: Assign unique user accounts instead of shared logins
Use least privilege with just-in-time or temporary elevation
explanation: MFA reduces risk for remote access. Unique accounts support accountability. Temporary elevation limits privileged exposure.

pbq.title: Print and application queue ticket
pbq.scenario: A support technician reviews three post-update issues. Match each symptom to the best first fix.

items.label: Print
items.value: Jobs stuck after spooler error

items.label: App
items.value: Crashes after plugin update

items.label: Service
items.value: Required service is set to disabled

question: Match each software symptom to the best first action.
itemsLeft: Print jobs are stuck after a spooler error
Application crashes immediately after a plugin update
itemsRight: Clear stuck jobs and restart the print spooler service
Disable/remove the plugin or restore the previous version
Set the service startup correctly and start the service
explanation: Print spooler errors often require clearing the queue and restarting the service. Plugin-related crashes should be isolated by disabling/removing the plugin. Disabled required services must be re-enabled.

pbq.title: Documentation handoff ticket
pbq.scenario: A technician closes three support tasks. Match each completed task to the documentation that should be updated.

items.label: Network fix
items.value: Static IP changed on a shared printer

items.label: Hardware swap
items.value: Laptop motherboard replaced under warranty

items.label: Security event
items.value: Phishing credential reset completed

question: Match each completed task to the best documentation update.
itemsLeft: Static IP changed on a shared printer
itemsRight: Update asset/network records and printer documentation
Update asset history, warranty/RMA notes, and serial details
Document incident ticket actions and user/security notifications
explanation: Shared network changes need accurate records. Hardware replacements affect asset/warranty history. Security events require incident documentation and notification trail.
